data.title: Software Stocks Are Plummeting… Are Their Forward Estimates Next?
data.description:
Artificial intelligence is shaking up the software industry. Indeed, lightning-fast advances from companies like OpenAI, Google (GOOGL), and Anthropic are leading to serious worries about how traditional software businesses will compete. As a result, former high-fliers such as Workday (WDAY) and Salesforce (CRM) are seeing sharp declines in their stock prices. Even so, many Wall Street analysts have been slow to adjust their earnings forecasts, which suggests that they may not fully appreciate the long-term disruption risk.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That gap becomes clear when looking at the numbers. According to Goldman Sachs (GS) strategist Ben Snider, software stocks tracked by the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) have fallen about 24% over the past three months. However, during that same period, two-year forward earnings estimates for the group have actually increased by 5%. Likewise, other industries seen as vulnerable to AI, including insurance brokers and advertising companies, have also received positive revisions to their 2026 earnings estimates.

As a result, some strategists believe that the gap will eventually close, and not in a good way. This is because historically, price action often reflects underlying risks before analysts adjust their forecasts. Software stocks that once traded at price-to-earnings multiples near 35 times are now below 20 times, according to Innovator Capital Management's Tim Urbanowicz, who spoke to Yahoo Finance. He also went on to say that this sell-off highlights the "dark side" of AI and serves as a warning that disruption could spread to other sectors as well.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Warren Buffett Opens Position in New York Times Co. (NYT) Stock, Cuts Amazon Stake
data.description:
Legendary investor Warren Buffett opened a new position in The New York Times Co.'s (NYT) stock in the fourth quarter of 2025 just before the shares rallied to an all-time high.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NYT stock was the only new position opened by Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) in the final quarter of last year. Buffett ran Berkshire for 60 years before stepping down as CEO on Dec. 31, 2025. He has been replaced at the helm by Greg Abel, who took over as CEO on Jan. 1.

It is not known if Buffett made the investment in The New York Times himself, or if it was one of the lieutenants who help to manage Berkshire's massive $317.46 billion stock portfolio. But Buffett has taken a shine to newspaper companies in the past. He was the largest outside shareholder and a board member of The Washington Post when it was a publicly traded company.

NYT stock rose 3% in after hours trading as news broke that Buffett has taken a stake in the company. Berkshire Hathaway bought 5.07 million shares of NYT stock worth $351.66 million during Q4 2025, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The New York Times position represents only 0.13% of Berkshire's equity portfolio.

Buffett's Other Moves

As is often the case, Berkshire Hathaway's investment came at an opportune time. NYT stock has rallied strongly in recent months. Shares of the newspaper company have risen 6% so far this year and are currently trading near an all-time high $74.59. The stock has gained 50% in the last 12 months as the news cycle has gone into overdrive with Donald Trump back in the White House.

Beyond The New York Times, Berkshire left most of its stock holdings unchanged in the final quarter of last year. However, the company did continue to trim its stakes in Apple (AAPL) and Bank of America (BAC), two of its largest holdings. Berkshire lowered its position in AAPL stock by 4% and cut its holdings of Bank of America by 9%.

In a surprise move, Berkshire reduced its holding in Amazon (AMZN) stock by 77% in the final months of 2025. That reduction also proved prescient with AMZN stock having declined 11% so far this year and in the midst of its biggest drawdown in 20 years. The only other significant move Berkshire made in Q4 2025 was to boost its holding of Domino's Pizza (DPZ) stock by 12%, taking its investment to $1.40 billion.

Is BRK.B Stock a Buy?

Only a couple of analysts currently offer a rating and price target on Berkshire Hathaway's more affordable Class B stock. So instead, we'll look at the shares' three-month performance. As one can see in the chart below, shares of BRK.B have declined 0.19% over the past 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: CHAT and WTAI: Two AI ETFs Offering High Dividend Yields
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) has become one of the key themes in the market, but not all AI ETFs are built the same. While many focus mainly on growth, two funds stand out for offering exposure to AI innovation and high dividend yields. These include the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) and the WisdomTree Artificial Intelligence & Innovation Fund (WTAI).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT)

The CHAT ETF has gained attention for its focus on companies driving generative AI, a fast-growing area fueled by large language models, AI-powered productivity tools, and new automation technologies.

What sets CHAT apart is its ability to combine fast-growing AI exposure with an unexpectedly strong dividend profile, giving investors a rare mix of innovation and income. The ETF has a dividend yield of 2.65%.

Some of the top holdings in the CHAT ETF include Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL). Overall, the ETF has $1.04 billion in assets under management (AUM) and an expense ratio of 0.75%. Over the past six months, the CHAT ETF has generated a return of 16.31%.

On TipRanks, CHAT has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. At $78.60, the average CHAT ETF price target implies 23.08% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Dan Ives Pounds the Table on These 3 Cyber Security Stocks
data.description:
Cyber security stocks have struggled in 2026 due to the AI-driven sell-off in software. However, five-star Wedbush analyst Dan Ives believes that this weakness could present a buying opportunity. In a recent note, he argued that artificial intelligence will ultimately act as a powerful tailwind for the sector. This is because as AI adoption expands across businesses, so does the need to protect data, applications, and endpoints.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, that view is based on the simple reality that hackers are also using AI, which is forcing companies to spend more on defense. According to Wedbush, some security vendors are actually raising their sales targets by as much as 30% this year due to strong demand. Even though IT budgets may face pressure, security spending is increasingly seen as non-negotiable. Still, bears argue that rising AI costs could crowd out other IT spending, thereby limiting overall growth. Nevertheless, Ives highlighted three companies that he believes are best positioned to benefit:

  • CrowdStrike (CRWD)
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW)
  • Zscaler (ZS)

Notably, he called CrowdStrike the industry's "gold standard" due to the strength of its Falcon platform, and maintained a Buy rating with a $600 price target. Secondly, Ives said that Palo Alto Networks remains a top pick, thanks to its platform consolidation strategy and its CyberArk acquisition, which earned the stock a $225 target. Finally, Zscaler received a $350 price target from Ives as a result of its durable subscription growth tied to its Zero Trust model.

Which Cyber Security Stock Is the Better Buy?

Overall, out of the three stocks mentioned above, analysts think that ZS stock has the most room to run. In fact, Zscaler's average price target of $315.24 per share implies 82.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.title: "46% Upside Potential": Here's Why This Top Analyst Is Bullish on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Citi analyst Atif Malik is bullish on Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report on February 25, pointing to strong product momentum and improving demand visibility that stretches well into 2027. He reiterated a Buy rating on NVDA stock and a $270 price target, which implies 46% upside potential.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The five-star analyst expects Nvidia to deliver another standout quarter, projecting January‑quarter revenue of $67 billion, comfortably above the Street's consensus of $65.6 billion. He also anticipates April‑quarter sales guidance of $73 billion, topping analyst estimates of $71.6 billion.

Analyst Sees Stronger Growth Ahead

Malik's optimism is driven by Nvidia's continued ramp of its B300 and Rubin platforms, which he believes will fuel a 34% acceleration in the second half of 2026 sales, compared with 27% growth in the first half.

Malik also forecasts Fiscal 2027 gross margins of around 75%. Moreover, he expects operating expense growth to remain in the high‑30% range, similar to Fiscal 2026.

He noted that most investors are already looking beyond the quarter toward Nvidia's annual GTC conference in mid-March, where the company is expected to outline its next-generation inference roadmap, including the use of Groq's low-latency SRAM IP.

Further, the analyst believes hyperscaler spending, while a concern for some investors, will ultimately deliver long-term returns as AI-driven infrastructure demand accelerates cloud revenue growth.

NVDA's Q4 Earnings and Revenue Expected to Jump Y/Y

Currently, analysts expect NVDA to post revenue of $65.58 billion in Q4, up from $39.33 billion in the year-ago quarter. Further, the company is expected to report earnings of $1.52 per share, compared with earnings of $0.89 in the prior-year quarter.

The market will be looking for updates on the rollout of Nvidia's latest platforms and any early signals about its 2026 product roadmap. Also, the company's guidance will play a major role in shaping sentiments around the broader AI and semiconductor sectors.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 379 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Intel Looks for "One in Two" PCs to be AI-Driven; Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips Regardless
data.description:
The notion of an artificial intelligence (AI) PC has caught some imaginations, but has not made a lot of headway in the market. But chip stock Intel (INTC) looks for the AI PC phenomenon to take off in a very big way, eventually. Intel looks for one out of every two PCs to be AI PCs, and it looks for Panther Lake to lead the way therein. Investors were skeptical. They sent Intel shares down fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, Intel is convinced that its Panther Lake lineup is perfect for the AI PC market. So much so, in fact, that with Panther Lake chips in these devices, their adoption rate would soar, ultimately reaching right around half of the overall PC market. Most AI PCs have suffered from a lack of overall computing power, but the Panther Lake lineup looks to deliver the necessary power, and thus improve adoption rates.

Intel Japan CEO Makoto Ohno noted, "It is also predicted that by 2026, this will exceed half, with one in two computers being an AI PC. However, given the current situation, reasons for choosing an AI PC include its high performance and the long battery life provided by the use of an NPU. In other words, it is important to reflect on the fact that people are not currently purchasing an AI PC in order to use its AI-related functions."

Xe Next

That is not all that is up Intel's collective sleeve, though. Intel is currently working on the Xe Next lineup, which is the next generation of graphics processing unit (GPU) property. It has no official name as yet, and there is not much information surrounding what it can do. But considering that it is set to be geared toward data centers, reports note, it suggests that Xe Next will be a significant power in the market.

But Xe Next will come after many of Intel's current products, like the Jaguar Shores lineup. Reports suggest that Jaguar Shores will be ready in the middle of the year, with a potential release either late this year, or early in 2027. And Intel is also working on further Arc releases, the Celestial and the Druid. Getting these into the field should augment Intel's performance at a time when it sorely needs new market share.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 70.83% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 3.57% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "The Solar Opportunity is Underestimated": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slides as Buffalo Workforce Ramping Up
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) may not be an electric vehicle giant much longer. It has made a lot of strides to diversify of late, and one of the big new things Tesla is pushing into is solar panels. In fact, reports suggest that Tesla's Buffalo workforce is getting some serious augmentation in the face of a potentially growing opportunity. Investors proved skeptical, though, judging by the nearly 2.5% drop in share prices in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla wants to roll out 100 gigawatts' worth of solar panels annually, reports note, a goal that is by any stretch ambitious. Tesla first got into solar when it acquired Solar City, but was only set to produce a bare hundredth of that number, just one gigawatt annually. But Tesla has been working, and recently revealed it has a new solar panel with not only more attractive looks, but also better performance in "partially shaded areas."

Tesla's own Elon Musk noted, "The solar opportunity is underestimated. We're gonna work towards getting 100 gigawatts a year of solar cell production, integrating across the entire supply chain, from raw materials all the way to finished solar panels." It would not be the first time a publicly-traded company went into solar panels, but few of them have done that well.

Kicking Tesla While It's Down

Many hope that Musk is right here, because the cars certainly have seen better days as far as sales go. A new story is going viral through Chinese state media about a Tesla Model Y that, somehow, managed to lose all power on the highway despite the fact it had significant range left to its battery, according to the cabin display.

The fact that Chinese media is state-controlled suggests that this story was deliberately amplified, reports note. This suggests a mood shift about Tesla in the mind of the Chinese government. Given how many of Tesla's competitors in the electric vehicle space are Chinese in nature, it is not outside the realm of possibility that the Chinese government may be taking a minor incident and using it as a goad to whip Tesla out of the market.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.88% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 1.9% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson
